text:
Five missiles struck Baghdad International Airport and injured four people, among them airport staff and security personnel, according to Lt. Gen Saad Maan, the head of the Iraqi Security Media Cell. The powerful Iran-backed Iraqi militia Kataib Hezbollah said it had launched the attack and that its rockets had struck Camp Victory, a former U.S. base within the airport complex that still hosts a diplomatic logistics hub.
In Lebanon, Israel has carried out more than 1,100 strikes since it launched a bombardment campaign against Hezbollah, the Iran-backed armed group, on March 2, said Shosh Bedrosian, a spokeswoman in the Israeli prime minister’s office.
Those include more than 200 strikes on missile launchers, 190 strikes on targets used by Hezbollah’s elite Radwan Force unit, and 35 “command and control sites.”
title: Iran War Live Updates: ‘No Guarantees’ Oil Prices Drop Soon, U.S. Official Says
